scouting_report: Safe Circulator - A possession-heavy center-back. Dominates in pass distance and safe short completions, but completely lacks progressive carrying or active defensive interventions (tackles/interceptions).
Clinical Poacher - A classic penalty-box striker. Highly efficient in shooting and goal/assist output per 90, but contributes absolutely nothing to build-up play, crossing, or dribbling.

Long-Range Shooter - A central player who shoots on sight. Takes a high volume of shots per 90 and overperforms expected assists, but avoids 1v1 dribbling and rarely touches the ball deep inside the box.
Direct Impact Player - An established starter who scores goals, but has remarkably low progressive distance and passing accuracy. Impacts the final third without participating in the build-up.
Transitional Relay - A standard, generalized midfielder. Shows slight progressive carrying tendencies but is highly reluctant to take on opponents in 1v1 situations. A safe, transitional player.

Progressive Runner - An elite forward who drives the ball forward. Generates massive expected goals by carrying the ball into dangerous areas, but completely avoids 1v1 dribbles and defensive tackling.

Flank Enforcer - A gritty, combative wide player. Exhibits extremely high defensive engagement in the midfield (tackles, yellow cards) and is prone to errors, lacking traditional winger traits like crossing.
Aggressive Maestro - A high-risk, fiery creator. Produces astounding expected assist (xA) numbers and finishing efficiency, but plays on the edge and is highly prone to red cards.
Press-Resistant Pivot - A deep midfielder who relies on technique rather than physical tackling. Excels at escaping pressure via dribbling and hitting long passes, but offers zero direct goal threat.

Deep Playmaker CB - An elite ball-playing defender. Provides an extraordinary number of assists and passes into the final third, acting almost as a deep-lying playmaker, but sacrifices traditional defensive grit.
Pure Destroyer - The ultimate midfield workhorse. Engages in a massive volume of challenges (both won and lost), acting as a pure physical shield with zero offensive progression or end-product.

Opportunistic Winger - An efficient, goal-scoring wide midfielder. Boasts solid goal output and expected assists, but rarely beats defenders on the dribble and is not heavily involved in receiving progressive passes.

Overlapping Anomaly - A bizarre defensive profile, likely overlapping wide center-backs. Crosses into the penalty area frequently but loses the ball often on the dribble and rarely tackles in their own third.

Direct Maverick - A highly progressive but erratic wide player. Produces incredible numbers for progressive passing and penalty kicks, but makes numerous errors and has terrible overall pass completion.
Conservative Anchor - A holding midfielder who plays it incredibly safe. Maintains high pass completion and decent challenge volumes but actively avoids progressive passing or forward risks.
Short-Pass Metronome - A conservative central player. Executes a high volume of safe, short passes and takes penalties, but almost never engages in dribbling or high-risk offensive actions.

Complete Target - The elite modern center-forward. Boasts exceptional non-penalty goal and assist numbers and constantly receives progressive passes in dangerous areas, without dropping deep to play long passes.

Set-Piece Threat - A center-back with incredibly high expected offensive output. Given their low carrying and tackling metrics, this is the classic 'aerial threat' who heavily impacts attacking corners.

Generational Finisher - Absolute superstar goalscorers. Show astronomical overperformance in goals vs xG. They do zero defensive blocking and have poor pass completion because every action is a high-risk shot at goal.

Dribbling Architect - An elite creative engine. Phenomenal at beating the press through dribbling and generating high-quality chances (xA) for teammates, though their personal shooting metrics are poor.
